content:
Incident Report
Page 4 of 11
scribe and log items of interest found in the vehicle while Detective Brown and Detective Robb photographed and inventoried the vehicle.
The vehicle was a marked police K-9 unit with an exempt license of XMT C8752C. There was a multi-caliber Daniel Defense rifle in the front seat of the patrol vehicle which bore serial number DDM4242680 and had a suppressor on the barrel. There was one chambered round in the rifle and 28 rounds in the magazine, which was inserted into the rifle. There was also an extra magazine for the rifle in the driver's side door which contained 29 rounds.
While searching the back hatch or trunk area of the vehicle we located the following items: Two partial boxes which contained Federal HST 9mm rounds with 36 rounds in one box and 47 rounds in the second box; what appeared to be a locked box which contained one NFDD, also referred to as a flash bang; a less lethal .12 gauge shotgun containing four beanbag style rounds in the magazine and six on the stock; four PMAGs for an AR-15 which were still sealed in the bag and empty; a .556 caliber magazine containing nine rounds; another .556 caliber magazine containing 12 rounds; another .556 caliber magazine containing 24 rounds; four .556 caliber magazines containing 29 rounds each; 108 loose .556 rounds; and 181 loose 9mm rounds. All of the other items located in the vehicle were general patrol items or items assoicated to a K-9.
We later searched an unmarked blue Dodge Charger, bearing Washington license AXR0804, which belonged to DOC Officer Whitehurst. We located seven loose .223 rounds in the center holder located in front of the shifter. After opening the trunk we located the following items: A ballistic shield; two new PMAGs for a . 223, which were still in the package and empty; a ram and Halligan tool; another empty .223 magazine; two 9mm pistol magazines containing 17 rounds each; and a box of loose and partially boxed ammunition, which contained in total 173 . 223 rounds and 114 9mm rounds .
Detectives then removed a camouflage backpack which contained the following 701 loose 9mm rounds; 268 loose . 223 rounds; and three .12 gauge shells. items:
At some point while doing an inventory of the vehicles, I saw members of the Thurston County Coroner's Office and other detectives photographing and removing the body of Michael Reinoehl. I was advised during this process it appeared that Mr. Reinoehl had a small caliber pistol in one of his pockets, which I did not personally witness.
After completing an inventory of the second vehicle, Lewis County detectives were advised we could clear the scene as the suspect vehicle was going to be impounded and processed at a later time.
I cleared the scene at approximately 0330 hours.
End of report.
I CERTIFY UNDER PENALTY OF PERJURY UNDER THE LAWS OF THE STATE OF WASHINGTON THAT ALL STATEMENTS MADE HEREIN ARE TRUE AND ACCURATE AND THAT I AM ENTERING MY AUTHORIZED USER ID AND PASSWORD TO AUTHENTICATE IT (RCW 9A. 72.085). Electronically Signed: Yes Signature: Kevin M. Engelbertson Chehalis/Lewis/Washington Date: 09-08-2020
rplwdir.x1
09/14/20
